verses:
gurudeva!
āmi svarūpataḥ, śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅yoṣita,
nivāsa⋅śrī⋅vraja⋅dhāma
hoiyā tomāra, jugala⋅sevāra,
sahāyatā⋅mora kāma

kothā sei kārya, kothā mui anārya,
jaḍa svārthe sadā ratā
tuccha sukha⋅svācchandya, sarvathā amanda,
rahe jadi hai kṛtārtha

bhāvi⋅kṛpā haila, janama saphala,
natubā hai je kātara
kṛṣṇa⋅vimukhatāya, kaṣṭa nāhi pāya,
citta kibā se pāthara

aho ki durgati ! kemana kumati !
jagate rahite daḍha
nija ṭhikānāya, mati nāhi dhāya,
mui chiḥ ki śocyatara !!

svarūpānubandhi⋅, sevā prati jadi,
śuddha lobha nāhi haya
koṭī janma dhari’, sukṛti vā kari,
tathāpi siddhi je naya

prabhu! ei nirbandha, jehetu sambandha,
āche mora tava sātha
jaḍa ākarṣaṇa, nāśi’ vikarṣaṇa,
deha’ sei lobha nātha !!

jāte vyākulatā, jāge mane sadā,
nityasevāya jogadāne
tabe je bhajana, habe anukṣaṇa,
druta gati vraja pāne

jogyatā⋅rahita, tabu āśā dīpta,
pā’ba ei kṛpā⋅dhana
bharasā āmāra, ‘śrī’⋅jana tomāra,
‘śrī’⋅sama navanī mana
en_translation:
O Gurudeva! By my original nature, I am a maidservant of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and my true home is the divine land of Vraja. Having become your follower, my only desire is to assist in the service of the Divine Couple.

Where is that divine service, and where am I—a low-born soul always absorbed in material self-interest? I would be fulfilled even by insignificant happiness and comfort, completely devoid of goodness.

I think that if mercy is granted, my life will be successful; otherwise, I will remain distressed. In my aversion to Lord Kṛṣṇa, I feel no pain—is my heart made of stone?

Oh, what a wretched state! What evil intelligence! I am firmly established in this world. My mind does not run toward my true address. Alas! How much more pitiable can I be?

If a pure greed for service, following one's true nature, does not arise, then even after countless births and performing many pious deeds, perfection is never attained.

O Lord! This persistent prayer is because of the relationship I have with you. Destroying my material attraction and repulsion, please grant me that spiritual greed, O Master!

May eagerness always awaken in my mind to participate in your eternal service. Then my devotional practice will be constant, making swift progress toward Vraja.

Though I am devoid of all qualifications, my hope shines brightly that I will obtain this treasure of mercy. My only hope is in your dear ones, whose minds are as soft as fresh butter.
